w:r.w:t: , an AI Agent Certification platform built to
evaluate and certify agent behaviour under controlled failure scenarios
Definition and execution of controlled failure experiments
External, non‑intrusive observability of agent behaviour
Objective scoring and benchmarking of agent outcomes
Certification evidence generation and audit trail
Runtime intervention or overriding agent decisions
Support for bring‑your‑own agents (no code instrumentation)
Detect reasoning failures early, not post‑deployment
Support governance, risk, and compliance reviews
Reduce reliance on anecdotal or manual validation
AI agent benchmarking for chaos engineering
with agent evaluation and observability layers
server and event-tracker use Go modules
Web app uses React, TS, webpack, Jest,
Go backend uses Mongo driver; Python side uses

; Go modules use k8s client libraries

config maps models through Azure endpoints

packaged in its own Docker image
Your scripts and current workflow rely on WSL +
Jest, Testing Library, Python schema smoke scripts
Frontend has Jest; repo has Python and shell validation scripts
Shell scripts, PowerShell scripts, Go tooling, Yarn/Webpack
, PowerShell start/stop scripts, Go generate/build, frontend yarn scripts
Secure public entry point for UI and APIs

Routes traffic from App Gateway into AKS workloads

Centralizes model access, retries, auth abstraction,
Production inference for agents and evaluation paths

Stores users, runs, experiment metadata, config
API keys, DB creds, JWT secret,
Infra and app monitoring for AKS workloads
, preferably on AKS or managed external deployment
Durable storage for generated outputs and archives
GitHub Actions or Azure DevOps + ACR + AKS deploy
SSO for operators and service identities
Locks down DB, Key Vault, and Azure OpenAI traffic
Whether its decisions meet defined safety and quality thresholds
No agent code or prompt changes
Separation of raw telemetry and derived scores
Full lineage from experiment → score → verdict
architecture to certify AI agent behaviour under failure.
